REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS WENDY HEMMING AND 55 NATOMA LLC'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER (YETIZEN LLC) IS DENIED. ATTACHMENT IS AN EXTRAORDINARY PROCEEDING AS TO WHICH THE MOVING PARTY MUST ESTABLISH, INTER ALIA, "THE PROBABLE VALIDITY OF THE CLAIM UPON WHICH AN ATTACHMENT MAY BE ISSUED." CCP 484.090. THIS IS A COMPLEX CASE INVOLVING ALLEGED BREACHES OF SEVERAL CONTRACTS IN CONNECTION WITH A COMMERCIAL LEASE. THE MOTION IS DENIED, AS THE MOVING PAPERS DO NOT MAKE CLEAR EVEN HOW MANY CONTRACTS ARE AT ISSUE, MUCH LESS STATE ALL CLAIMS OR SHOW THEIR PROBABLE VALIDITY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Caitlin Connell, Esq. (925-600-1800) for Plaintiff/ opposing party WEndy Hemming.
Ann Draper, Esq. (415-981-5620) for Defendant/Moving party Naveen Singha.

The Court admonishes parties' tardiness. The Court announces the tentative ruling and the reasons for it. The Court has concluded Plaintiff's counsel did know the Defendant was not at the address and did not show due diligence on serving the notice. ARGUED. Plaintiff argues due diligence. Defendant argues Plaintiff should have dug deeper. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Off Calendar Dec-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Caitlin Connell, Esq. of Patton & Sullivan LLP, (925-600-1800), for Wendy He
Kevin F. Rooney, Esq. of Browne George Ross, (415-391-7100), for Defendants and opposing parties.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFs WENDY HEMMING and 55 NATOMA LLC's Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order (Sana Choudary) is DENIED. Attachment is an extraordinary proceeding as to which the moving party must establish, inter alia, "the probable validity of the claim upon which an attachment may be issued." CCP 484.090. This is a complex case involving alleged breaches of several contracts in connection with a commercial lease. The motion is DENIED, as the moving papers do not make clear even how many contracts are at issue, much less state all claims or show their probable validity. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
